findings.finding: Excellent Spring Boot introduction. 6 content blocks covering auto-configuration, starter dependencies.
findings.details: Good explanation of problems Spring Boot solves. Warning correctly notes Jakarta migration.

findings.finding: Warning explicitly mentions 'javax.* imports are jakarta.* (this was done in Spring Boot 3.0)'
findings.details: This shows content is aware of Jakarta EE transition. Good for learners.

findings.finding: Complete REST controller coverage. 6 content blocks with annotations and response patterns.
findings.details: @RestController, @RequestMapping, @GetMapping, @PostMapping all covered correctly. Status code handling included.

findings.lessonTitle: Spring Data JPA - No More SQL
findings.finding: Comprehensive Spring Data JPA. 7 content blocks covering repositories, query methods, relationships.
findings.details: Uses JpaRepository correctly. JPQL and native query examples accurate. Lazy loading warning included.

findings.lessonTitle: Spring Data JPA - No More SQL
findings.finding: JPA annotations use correct modern patterns (@Entity, @Table, @Id, @GeneratedValue)
findings.details: No deprecated Hibernate-specific annotations found. Standard JPA throughout.

findings.lessonTitle: Dependency Injection - The Heart of Spring
findings.finding: Excellent DI explanation. 8 content blocks covering constructor injection, @Autowired, @Service, @Repository.
findings.details: Correctly promotes constructor injection over field injection. Layered architecture explanation is sound.

findings.finding: Comprehensive configuration coverage. 15 content blocks - most extensive lesson in module.
findings.details: application.properties, YAML, profiles, @Value, @ConfigurationProperties, environment variables all covered.

findings.finding: Complete exception handling. 10 content blocks with @ControllerAdvice, @ExceptionHandler, custom exceptions.
findings.details: Proper error response structure. ProblemDetail (RFC 7807) mentioned. Validation integration included.

findings.finding: Modern Spring Security 7 patterns. 12 content blocks covering SecurityFilterChain, authentication, authorization.
findings.details: Uses SecurityFilterChain bean (not deprecated WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter). Lambda DSL throughout.

findings.finding: Lesson explicitly mentions 'Spring Security 7' and SecurityFilterChain
findings.details: Challenge explanation notes: 'Spring Security 7 uses SecurityFilterChain beans with lambda DSL. WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter was removed in Spring Security 6.'

findings.finding: Uses modern authorizeHttpRequests() and requestMatchers() (not deprecated antMatchers)
findings.details: All examples use lambda DSL: .authorizeHttpRequests(auth -> auth...). No deprecated chaining syntax.

findings.finding: @EnableWebSecurity used correctly (still required in Spring Security 7)
findings.details: Challenge correctly states WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter was removed in Spring Security 6.
